differential_diagnoses: Penetrating duodenal ulcer causing retroperitoneal abscess and duodeno-renal fistula
Streptococcus anginosus group liver abscess with portal/renal vein thrombophlebitis
Severe Strongyloidiasis (hyperinfection syndrome) with secondary bacterial sepsis
Infective endocarditis with septic pulmonary and renal emboli
Duodenal malignancy with localized perforation and secondary infection
next_steps: Perform urgent upper endoscopy (EGD) to evaluate duodenal wall thickening and possible ulcer/fistula
Repeat blood cultures to monitor for clearance of Streptococcus anginosus
Continue broad-spectrum antibiotics (Ceftriaxone or similar, possibly adding Metronidazole) based on S. anginosus susceptibility
Maintain therapeutic anticoagulation for inferior vena cava and renal vein thrombosis
Consider echocardiography to rule out vegetation associated with persistent bacteremia
Order stool studies for Strongyloides stercoralis larvae (O&P and serology)
